title: Is Rei actually 10 11
wikitext:
[]: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Memer557 » Thu Nov 26, 2020 4:35 am I didn’t think of it until now but in the original series little Rei pops up out of nowhere after Shinji is sent away which judging how Gendo is acting has been a few months meaning by the time Rei 1 dies (by Naoko) she is technically 6/7 instead of 10 and by the time the anime starts is 10/11 and the reason I say this is cause gendo cloned yui after she died and is probably is one of the reasons why we don’t actually know her really birthday Ps feel free to bash me with logic if I’m wrong

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Berserker » Thu Nov 26, 2020 7:23 am Physically no, mentally yes.We do have a wiki and it has answers to simple question like these. EvaWiki wrote: Rei Ayanami was created sometime between 2004 and 2010, as suggested by her appearance in Episode 21. Seen in 2010, she has the physical appearance of a small child, perhaps around four or five years old. She is later seen in 2015 apparently aged 14 (she would have to appear the same age as Shinji's classmates since they are in the same class). Since Rei was created after Yui died and none of her bodies can possibly be 14 years old, it's implicit that Gendo and Fuyutsuki can manipulate her growth.

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Nov 26, 2020 9:54 am We know she joined Kensuke's and Touji's class the year before. (as mentioned in ep 5) Obviously they must have given her some degree of "programming" She'd be useless as a weapon if you had to potty train her every time you needed a new clone. They seem to "backup" her memories (Rei III had a gap where the battle was, but still knew who Shinji & the others are - it's unclear if she'd have any memories from Rei I or if they only worked out the memory transfer tech later for the dummy plug) Ultimately it's not time passing, but the hormonal changes that make your brain go from child state to young adult state (nor would the process necessarily be completely finished in a 14 year old; Maybe that's part why the pilots have to be so young, so the brains are still "adaptable"; If you wanted max adaptability you could use a toddler, but good luck getting one to follow orders) so I wouldn't necessarily conclude that this means she would be mentally immature - If anything she acts the most mature out of the pilots. (see Kensuke's analysis in ep 14) She's obviously mentally sophisticated, intellectual even - reading complicated texts and even philosophizing. Regardless of what, say, Ritsuko or Asuka believe about her, her behavior doesn't actually suggest that she just mechanically follows orders. Rather she seems to actually believe in Gendo's cause and will smack you if you insult him. Her perspective is obviously still influenced by what he tells you, but no more than anyone else would be influenced by the culture/religion they were brought up in. She's a homeschooled kid, not a robot; She consistently shows initiative (at first largely in volunteering for stuff, running off on her own in eps 16 and 19), concern for her fellow pilots (Asuka & Touji included; But she goes to sit at Shinji's bedside before she even gets to know him) and isn't unscrupulous either; Bardiel defeats her because she hesitates to blast Touji. Though of course you could speculate about the effects this would have, ie that she perhaps missed out on some of the things that ppl typically learn as toddlers - bonding, implicit tone etc. A baby would actually not develop correctly if they don't get nurturing, touch & affection, there's been cases of kids who spent much time in hospitals or crappy orphanages who end up quiet and closed-off cause they didn't have adequate mirroring, though normally this would also come with some degree of impulsivity & cognitive delay; They didn't need to worry about that 'cause they probably popped a ready made 12 year old from the tube, probably designed to be self-sufficient with all skills needed to live on her own, but that essentially let them get away with a level of neglect that a "conventionally grown" kid would not have survived.

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by orcot » Thu Nov 26, 2020 12:05 pm Rei's age is complicated because of the reasons stated before, also what if Rei 3 is the combination of Rei2 and unit 00? The Rei "series" for a better word is 6/7 years old artificialy aged but that seemed to have happenend in the reiquarium as all Rei's look the same age. Naoko killed Rei 1 in 2008. This is probably the split between Rei 1 into Rei 2 and possible unit 00, so if you count both their experiences Rei 3 might actualy be a max of 12 (6+6) (in 2014) but it could be as little as 2 years old (first time Rei 2 has been confirmed was when she entered school). It could also be said with lilith soul that she is billion's of years old or hyperfocused on Rei 3 roughly 1 month old. Then theirs the fact that Kendrix is right and a baby would not mentaly develop this way if you artificialy aged it so their might actually be even more going on in her As I said multiple options.

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Berserker » Thu Nov 26, 2020 1:04 pm orcot wrote: Naoko killed Rei 1 in 2008. This is probably the split between Rei 1 into Rei 2 and possible unit 00, so if you count both their experiences Rei 3 might actualy be a max of 12 (6+6) (in 2014) but it could be as little as 2 years old (first time Rei 2 has been confirmed was when she entered school). It could also be said with lilith soul that she is billion's of years old or hyperfocused on Rei 3 roughly 1 month old.

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by orcot » Thu Nov 26, 2020 1:44 pm Not really, more experience connected to a body. The body of Rei 3 never met Shinji yet she knows him, theirs a transfer of some sort. If not, your in the camp that Rei 3 is only like a month old but then it's weird she picked Shinji as they have only talked a couple of times never abouth anything substantial and Shinji is actively avoiding her. If Rei has 2 bodies at the same time I don't see why you can't add those experiences all up together. Like Rei spent at least 2 years going to junior high school and her filthy apartment, she also spent 2 years chillaxing in a coldbath and getting brutally tortured and turnend into a fighting machine those experiencesDepending on your point of view you might add them up. (Say you are born in 2000 in 2020 you invent a machine to fuse yourself with a alternate reality you or say your partner (you have the same age in this example) are you now 20 (both where 20 when they fused but lived a different life). Or are you alltogether a other lifeform that is either 20 or 40 years old? That's sort of the problem with Rei, Rei can genuily be both 1month;2Y;8Y;12Y;+billionY depending on your point of view at the end of evangelion That said kendrix is right as well I think

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Line » Thu Nov 26, 2020 2:49 pm This discussion remind me, a post I saw once in tumblr who was wondering the question do Rei age? Like, here a mindfucking thinking : Did Rei II as been born as being a 8-10 kid aged girl, or do she was directly brought to this world as a 14 one?

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Nov 26, 2020 3:46 pm Line wrote: This discussion remind me, a post I saw once in tumblr who was wondering the question do Rei age? Like, here a mindfucking thinking : Did Rei II as been born as being a 8-10 kid aged girl, or do she was directly brought to this world as a 14 one?

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by orcot » Thu Nov 26, 2020 3:56 pm The reiquarium tells us that all Rei's are roughly the same age theirs no significant age difference between them and Rei 2,3. However they got artificialy aged it probably happenend in the Reiquarium. Rei's 2 extra medical test might be active test because she is either conscience and can complain (tell where it hurts) and they extrapolate to her clonegals. Or it involved her serious injury from her fall. I don't think the pills age her faster or something. Or a precousion because she has to pilot dangerous equipment (don't really believe that because we don't see Shinji or Asuka getting the same treatment but they might get a similar treatment and simply not show that) @kendrix I doubt kaworu is inmortal but he plays according to cylons rules killing him yust releases his soul and he finds a other vessel (like happenend in the series) Rei didn't only consume adams flesh but also his soul you can see it in the double spirit of Rei attached to kaworu . Abouth the never aging part I also have my doubts didn't they say they poured adams soul in a human vessel? if it's human it will age eventually I assume (do tell if I'm missing something)

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Line » Thu Nov 26, 2020 5:47 pm I ask the question because when Rei I die is she is directly remplaced by another clone? Or did they wait 2015 or two or tree years before?... Considering the personnality of Gendo, now that's I think about it, it's unlikely that's he did wait this long to product another clone who is basically the subsistute of his wife/daughter -to him.- And Ritsuko said "This is the place where Rei grow up" so maybe, clearly to me, she did not come out as a 14 years old already unlike Rei III. I dunno, if Rei II has been made quickly after Rei I death, it would be too weird to me, to directly age her up to 14 via scien fi technology and so she would be 14 for a decenny? This make no sense to me if that's the case. Sorry if I sound awfully dumb.

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Berserker » Fri Nov 27, 2020 1:55 am But please watch your summary Rei is most definitly artificially aged therefore not entirly biological

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Nov 27, 2020 3:21 am Now were getting into semantics really & what you mean by "program" It is suggested that there's some overlap in the technology used in the magi vs. the dummy plugs (id have to search the booklets and the CI for the exact quote, something about "research into artificial souls"), but since Naoko was chunky salsa by the time Rei II was created, the implication is that Ritsuko did it later on. I don't think "they just taught her everything normally" is a possibility (Rei II patently does not have the vocabulary or decision making process of a 4 year old)

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by orcot » Fri Nov 27, 2020 3:41 am @kendrix we don't really know how she got the memories it's a mystery anyone's guess is as good as a other, in a private post to berserker I mentionend Rei could have consumed the soul of some 5 year old that had the ability to do these things. I'm not saying that is what happenend theirs, just that theirs alternatives. In short we don't know. But if theirs word of god in some article please do post it Berserker touched on that Rei's age could be a Ship of Theseus (if I understood him)

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Berserker » Fri Nov 27, 2020 4:58 am Rei could have consumed the soul of some 5 year old that had the ability to do these things.

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by IgRAzm » Fri Nov 27, 2020 10:26 am It seems to me that most commonly accepted concept is this: Lilith's body we see is soulless, and the entire soul was passed over to Rei, it's just that the spiritual connection between the soul and body still remains. This small element doesn't disregard the entire idea, and at some point I myself thought that soul leaving a piece of it in Lilith makes more sense, until I've got more convinced that a god-like being, assumedly equal to Adam in strength, would probably be more active at fighting its one and only enemy, the Angels, and the lack of a soul explains it's lifelessness, at least. I think orcot misses the point that souls aren't the main component that carries people's memories, based on the evidence we have that would be brain. But there is still something that souls do carry over that connects to memories, and it's probably strong emotions. Or, at least, something that associates in people with strong emotional reactions, preferably those meaningful to them. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by orcot » Fri Nov 27, 2020 11:37 am @IgRAzm (soul/brain stuff) In real life yes but remember episode 20, Shinji is dissolved (including his brain) afther his fight with zeruel and he is doing relatively fine, he can think remember etc. So in the NGE universe the soul can hold those memories and properties that in real life happens in the brain. (that said Naoko shows us that the (NGE) brain is most definitely used for information processing). this part is a bit off topic but here's my respons - Rei's ending in the series is rather sad one of the reasons she cries during that scene is because she doesn't know her own emotions basicly she cries because she doesn't understand why she cries. But that's a bid off topic - Also if it was that easy the would have euthanised Rei II the moment she hot her accident under ideal conditions, nobody would have know the extent of her injuries and she could have gone back to school as Rei III without anyone knowing the difference.Also if you would go as far as making a back up of her mind one hour before the test would have been a good time. - Naoko knew 30 seconds into meeting Rei that she was a clone of Yui, whilst Naoko is a genius, you can't ignore that Naoko is more or less a loner who is seen constantly working alone ignoring her daughter etc so in facial/voice recognition she is probably below average. (she identifies as a scientist a mother and a woman, why not as a human being?) So anyone who have either seen of heard Yui can peace it together rather quickly. - Also most of the eva prep you talk abouth was done by Yui not Naoko, by Yui's death/ascention the already had unit 00 and a version of unit 01 (still attached to lilith, entry plugs, plug suits etc. Naoko was more involved with the MAGI and advanced biotech computers and only serious to project E afther the Yui event to retrieve her. She failed whilst Fuyutsuki managed to get a compromise also this happenend in 2004 Naoko met Rei for the first time in 2010. And first mistaked her for Shinji a boy that should be 9 by now. Fuyutsuki and Rei have the weirdest relation in the entire show as he obviously created her from a women that he "loved". Rei on her part not only seems interested in Fuyutsuki field of expertiece, he also joinend NERV, because "to protect of the future of humanity" so he has a similar general philosophy and outlook on life with Rei (compared to Gendo's nihilistic as long as I am reconnected with my wife nigilisme). They also share a similar job and work enviroment but it is never acknowledged or even mentionend and the pass eachother as strangers. To beserker I believe she has all of lilith soul, I also do not believe Lilith is potty trainend because she neither wears clothes, drinks or responds to anything (with the exception of the spear). Meaning Rei has all of the soul (initially)

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by Berserker » Sat Nov 28, 2020 12:57 am Well, i agree with IgRAzm mostly, i also said to orcot to not to take this into spiritual side as that has nothing to do with the main topic and it'll complexate the matter more and more. But here we go.... First of all, orcot please stay with a single decision. One minute you're saying Rei I "consumed" a random 5 years old's soul, the next it's Lilith's entire soul. If you agree with someone, say so. But the way you're changing your decision is making everything worse. Now, it's kinda funny how you guys managed to create a paradox in this. If you take Rei I's soul as whole Lilith's soul and soul capable of storaging memory, then it's entirely contradictory as Rei I or Rei III didn't have a single memory of Lilith whatsoever, but Kaworu did as he's obviously the vessel of Adam's entire soul. On the contrary, if soul's incapable of storaging memory and Rei I had Lilith's whole soul, then it makes sense on the case of Rei not having any memory of Lilith, but it's incorrect as Kaworu doesn't have Adam's brain while having Adam's whole memory. About Lilith's body being soulless: The lack of soul is obviously taken as "dead" status. Then it's a stretch as dead body doesn't bleed or regenerate limbs. The lifelessness and unresponsive position was also seen on the picture where eva 1 was still attached with Lilith, still having her whole soul at that moment. It can be her will or the lance. So in that case, the body should at least have a single part of the soul to stay "alive to bleed and regenerate legs". Remember, Rei was completely Lilith when her soul merged with Lilith's soul in EoE. Until then, she was Rei. Even if Rei I had Lilith's whole soul or not, she had the necessity of teaching her the basic things to be human like eating, reading, and of course shitting. Again, she didn't have memory of Lilith.Even if she did, these things are human lessons, taught from human by human. It's comprehensively possible for Gendo, Naoko or Fuyutsuki or a random scientist they ordered to teach her these things. My opinion is Gendo, as she spend her most time with him, learned the "old hag" word unintentionally from him(possible hint), most probably lived with him. He certainly didn't do a great job, but did something rather than nothing. And Gendo obviously did have or at least acted like he had a soft spot for Rei, which most fathers have for their daughters more comparing their relation with sons. orcot wrote: episode 20, Shinji is dissolved (including his brain) afther his fight with zeruel and he is doing relatively fine, he can think remember etc. So in the NGE universe the soul can hold those memories and properties that in real life happens in the brain.

[]: Re: Is Rei actually 10/11             Quote Post by IgRAzm » Fri Nov 27, 2020 10:26 am It seems to me that most commonly accepted concept is this: Lilith's body we see is soulless, and the entire soul was passed over to Rei, it's just that the spiritual connection between the soul and body still remains. This small element doesn't disregard the entire idea, and at some point I myself thought that soul leaving a piece of it in Lilith makes more sense, until I've got more convinced that a god-like being, assumedly equal to Adam in strength, would probably be more active at fighting its one and only enemy, the Angels, and the lack of a soul explains it's lifelessness, at least. I think orcot misses the point that souls aren't the main component that carries people's memories, based on the evidence we have that would be brain. But there is still something that souls do carry over that connects to memories, and it's probably strong emotions. Or, at least, something that associates in people with strong emotional reactions, preferably those meaningful to them. SPOILER: Show

Article: Is Rei actually 10 11
RAzm mostly, i also said to orcot to not to take this into spiritual side as that has nothing to do with the main topic and it'll complexate the matter more and more. But here we go.... First of all, orcot please stay with a single decision. One minute you're saying Rei I "consumed" a random 5 years old's soul, the next it's Lilith's entire soul. If you agree with someone, say so. But the way you're changing your decision is making everything worse. Now, it's kinda funny how you guys managed to create a paradox in this. If you take Rei I's soul as whole Lilith's soul and soul capable of storaging memory, then it's entirely contradictory as Rei I or Rei III didn't have a single memory of Lilith whatsoever, but Kaworu did as he's obviously the vessel of Adam's entire soul. On the contrary, if soul's incapable of storaging memory and Rei I had Lilith's whole soul, then it makes sense on the case of Rei not having any memory of Lilith, but it's incorrect as Kaworu doesn't have Adam's brain while having Adam's whole memory. About Lilith's body being soulless: The lack of soul is obviously taken as "dead" status. Then it's a stretch as dead body doesn't bleed or regenerate limbs. The lifelessness and unresponsive position was also seen on the picture where eva 1 was still attached with Lilith, still having her whole soul at that moment. It can be her will or the lance. So in that case, the body should at least have a single part of the soul to stay "alive to bleed and regenerate legs". Remember, Rei was completely Lilith when her soul merged with Lilith's soul in EoE. Until then, she was Rei. Even if Rei I had Lilith's whole soul or not, she had the necessity of teaching her the basic things to be human like eating, reading, and of course shitting. Again, she didn't have memory of Lilith.Even if she did, these things are human lessons, taught from human by human. It's comprehensively possible for Gendo, Naoko or Fuyutsuki or a random scientist they ordered to teach her these things. My opinion is Gendo, as she spend her most time with him, learned the "old hag" word unintentionally from him(possible hint), most probably lived with him. He certainly didn

 random scientist they ordered to teach her these things. And Gendo obviously did have or at least acted like he had a soft spot for Rei, which most fathers have for their daughters more comparing their relation with sons. orcot wrote: episode 20, Shinji is dissolved (including his brain) afther his fight with zeruel and he is doing relatively fine, he can think remember etc. So in the NGE universe the soul can hold those memories and properties that in real life happens in the brain.
